vitals.location: Viana do Castelo, Alto Minho, Portugal
vitals.field_size: 1,775 entry cap across all distances; ~1,900 estimated participants from 22 nationalities in 2026
vitals.entry_fee: EUR43 (Portuguese Cycling Federation-licensed riders) / EUR47 (non-federated, incl. all foreign entrants)
vitals.feed_zones: Multiple aid stations along the route (exact count not published)

climate.description: Mid-March in Alto Minho: cool, Atlantic-influenced, with a real chance of rain and wind off the coast.
challenges: March Atlantic weather — cool temperatures and rain/wind risk are common

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 59. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 57. SOL REVIEW FLAG (2026-07-24, not applied — numeric dimensions locked per wave-10 rule): reviewer notes distance=4 for 130km is generous under the repo rubric (implies 3), and climate_risk/scenic_experience at 4 and organization at 3 are not clearly evidenced by the research dump for this open-road, chip-timed format. Flagging for a human rescoring pass, not auto-applying.

course_description.character: A distinctly Alto Minho course: green Atlantic valleys, an inland mountain section, and a fast coastal return. The Granfondo skirts Serra d'Arga through Amonde, Meixedo and Lanheses, climbs Cabracao (3km at 6.6%), then extends toward Paredes de Coura through Agualonga, Rubiaes, Sapardos and Covas before its principal late difficulty — the climb to Sopos (4.5km at 6.2%) — ahead of Vilar de Mouros, Venade, Caminha and the return to Viana. It's rolling-to-mountainous rather than alpine or genuinely mountainous: repeated transitions between the Lima and Minho valleys and the Serra d'Arga/Formigoso foothills matter more than any single summit. The roads are open to traffic under the Highway Code, not fully closed.
course_description.signature_challenge: The late climb to Sopos (4.5km at 6.2%) — the harder of the route's two named ascents and the last major test before the run back to Viana.
suffering_zones: Cabracao (3km at 6.6%) — the first real test, early enough to sort the field before the Paredes de Coura loop
Sopos (4.5km at 6.2%) — the closing climb, hitting riders late with the coastal finish still ahead
Open-road sections through farm and mountain villages — no full closures, so Highway Code traffic rules apply throughout

logistics.lodging_strategy: Viana do Castelo is a mid-size coastal city with standard tourist accommodation.

history.origin_story: Launched 19 March 2023 by organizer Bikeservice as part of Viana do Castelo's year as European City of Sport, debuting with 131/84/62km routes through Viana do Castelo, Ponte de Lima, Paredes de Coura and Caminha, and drawing an estimated 1,700-2,000 riders in its first edition.
notable_moments: Reached its fourth edition in 2026, now serving as the season-opening round of Bikeservice's Superprestigio amateur series (turnout has held in the 1,700-1,900 range since its 2023 debut, without an established growth trend)
history.reputation: A young Alto Minho fondo with a strong, consistent debut-level turnout and a genuinely rolling-to-mountainous course for the region — its own published numbers, however, don't always agree with each other.

biased_opinion.verdict: Young, Scenic, Slightly Sloppy on Paperwork
biased_opinion.summary: Viana Granfondo is a real event — launched in 2023 for Viana do Castelo's European City of Sport year, it drew an estimated 1,700-2,000 riders in its debut and has held similar turnout (~1,900 in 2026) through its fourth edition, now anchoring Bikeservice's Superprestigio amateur series. The course earns its Alto Minho setting: two named climbs (Cabracao and Sopos) bookending a rolling-to-mountainous loop through the Lima and Minho valleys with a coastal finish. What it hasn't nailed down is its own numbers — official sources disagree on the shorter routes' distances (53/99km on one page, 60/80km on the regulation PDF), the published entry cap (1,775) doesn't obviously reconcile with a reported ~1,900 participants, and post-race coverage has reported the Granfondo itself at 127km, 129km, and the advertised 130km in different places.
strengths: Genuinely varied Alto Minho terrain with two named, graded climbs (Cabracao 3km/6.6%, Sopos 4.5km/6.2%)
Consistent turnout since its 2023 debut, drawing riders from 22 nationalities by 2026
Now the season-opening round of an established regional amateur series (Bikeservice Superprestigio)
Scenic coastal-to-mountain route touching Ponte de Lima, Paredes de Coura and Caminha
weaknesses: Materially inconsistent published course data across the organizer's own pages and post-race reports — verify current-year distances before you commit to pacing plans
Published entry cap (1,775) and reported participant count (~1,900) don't cleanly reconcile — likely different count definitions, but the organizer doesn't explain the gap
Open roads, not full closures — Highway Code traffic rules apply for the whole route
A March date means real weather risk (cold, wind, rain) on the Atlantic coast
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A legitimate, scenic early-season option if you're chasing Superprestigio points or want a rolling-to-mountainous Alto Minho fondo — just don't trust the published km/elevation/field-size numbers to the decimal, and dress for March Atlantic weather.

final_verdict.one_liner: A young, scenic Alto Minho fondo with real climbing and a habit of publishing conflicting numbers.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes, if you want an early-season European fondo with genuine hills and coastal scenery — just confirm the current year's official course figures directly rather than trusting any single published source.
final_verdict.alternatives: For a more established, better-documented Portuguese road fondo, compare against other Bikeservice Superprestigio rounds later in the same series calendar.

videos.title: Granfondo VIANA DO CASTELO | Aventura de Ciclismo no coração da cidade
videos.description:
VIANA GRANFONDO 🇵🇹

1700 ciclistas num Granfondo que teve uma pitada muito especial, largamos entre os 100 primeiros e pudemos sentir a emoção de andar no meio de um pelotão cheio de feras!

Um desafio à altura das belas paisagens que se encontram pelo caminho, cada obstáculo ultrapassado era premiado com uma vista de tirar o fôlego!

Três percursos de diferentes graus de dificuldade mas todos eles capazes de deixar belíssimas recordações. 

São eles: o Granfondo, com 131 km; o Mediofondo, com 84 km; e o Minifondo, com 62 km.

Os percursos, com partida e chegada à cidade de Viana do Castelo, passando por concelhos como Ponte de Lima, Paredes de Coura ou Caminha, oferecem paisagens únicas de mar e serra, assim como um riquíssimo património cultural de onde há a destacar, entre outros, o Templo do Sagrado Coração de Jesus, no monte de Santa Luzia, ou o Santuário de Nossa Senhora das Neves, em Caminha.

“Vamos conquistar o coração do Minho”

Um grande abraço para todos.
